claim: Feedback loops (evals, A/B tests, vibes) are the critical infrastructure for AI development, not the specific mechanism; best teams deliberately invest in all three types with appropriate effort allocation
actionable: Stop debating evals terminology; instead, audit your feedback loop infrastructure across offline evals, online A/B tests, and qualitative vibes checks; invest effort where ROI is highest
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: Golden dataset manufacturing is obsolete; modern evals capture real production failures and pull them into offline environments for iteration, not upfront enumeration of test cases
actionable: Monitor production logs for user failures daily; pull novel failure modes into your offline eval suite within 24h rather than pre-manufacturing test datasets
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: Code generation is uniquely verifiable compared to other AI tasks (does it compile/render?) enabling RL fine-tuning to fix trivial errors 100x faster than agentic loops
actionable: For code generation: run trivial evals (compilation, rendering) then feed results to fine-tuned models to fix 30-900 tokens/sec vs agentic approaches
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: Evals enable velocity by making the 'first derivative' (whether a change improves things) observable, allowing aggressive iteration without fear of regression—analogous to unit tests in traditional software
actionable: If you can't measure whether a prompt change improved your system, you're forced into conservative, slow iteration; instrument evals to know your first derivative
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: Evals as product management: LLM-as-judge rubrics let non-technical product managers encode domain expertise into scoring functions, replacing vague PRDs with measurable criteria
actionable: Have product managers write eval rubrics (scoring functions) instead of 50-page PRDs; let engineers verify compliance with automated checks rather than interpretation
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: Vibes-based feedback (qualitative judgement by domain experts) is actually a form of evaluation—just an expensive, non-scalable one—not an alternative to evals
actionable: Recognize vibes as valid feedback but budget accordingly; if you're relying on expert vibes, don't pretend it scales—formalize it into rubrics or scale with automated evals
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: Public benchmarks (marketing evals) are orthogonal to private feedback loop evals; a company can benefit from evals internally without publishing benchmarks, and published benchmarks don't improve product iteration velocity
actionable: Build evals optimized for YOUR feedback loop, not for publishable leaderboards; separate the effort of marketing communication from the effort of iteration velocity
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: RL environments can encode domain expertise at scale without requiring domain experts; a published RL environment is more efficient than attempting to upload proprietary data to model labs
actionable: For domain-specific AI tasks, consider publishing RL environments (e.g., framework benchmarks) rather than sharing proprietary eval data with model providers
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl

claim: Brittle evals tied to implementation details slow iteration; good evals target the true north star of the problem so they remain relevant as systems evolve
actionable: When designing evals, focus on the business outcome (e.g., 'user completes their task') not implementation detail (e.g., 'specific model generates X output'); brittle evals become anchors
source_title: The Great Evals Debate — Ankur Goyal & Malte Ubl
